title: import: write stdin to tmp file
body:
If importing an archive via stdin write it to a temporary file such that
the temporary file can be opened multiple times later on.  Otherwise, we
may end up with an empty image.

Add system tests for `podman import` exercising the basics.

Fixes: #10994
Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <rothberg@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@edsantiago PTAL at the new system test.  I had the thought of turning it into table-driven tests but found it simpler like that.

@rhatdan @TomSweeneyRedHat shall we create a BZ for this one?

It's a regression in 3.2 where archives cannot be imported via stdin.
If we want to get it fixed in rhel8.4.0.2 then it needs a bugzilla.

> If we want to get it fixed in rhel8.4.0.2 then it needs a bugzilla.

Do you want it fixed in 8.4.0.2?
I am not sure if there is time.  If so then yes.

Tests LGTM, and I agree that there's no sane way to write them in a table or for-loop.

I have one enormous concern: your new test passes on podman *without* your patch:
```console
$ git co main
$ make
...
$ git co pr/11018
$ ./bats import
# bats  test/system/125-import.bats
 ✓ podman import

1 test, 0 failures

--------------------------------------------------
$ bats  test/system/125-import.bats
 ✓ podman import

1 test, 0 failures
```
It also passes with `PODMAN=/usr/bin/podman ./bats import` (podman-3.2.2-1.fc34). This means that your test is not testing what you think it is testing.

I tried the obvious fix:
```diff
-    run_podman import -q - < $archive
+    run_podman import -q - <(cat $archive)
```
...but that just causes podman (unpatched & patched) to hang. Probably one of those BATS weirdnesses. I need to think about this. For now, please do not merge.

~~Suggestion for test:~~
[DELETED. Will add new comment with a better way]

Solved it! This is the solution:
```diff
diff --git a/test/system/125-import.bats b/test/system/125-import.bats
index 596884668..c53711618 100644
--- a/test/system/125-import.bats
+++ b/test/system/125-import.bats
@@ -25,7 +25,7 @@ load helpers
     run_podman rmi -f $iid

     # Simple import via stdin
-    run_podman import -q - < $archive
+    run_podman import -q - < <(cat $archive)
     iid="$output"
     run_podman run -t --rm $iid cat /random.txt
     is "$output" "$random_content" "simple import via stdin"
@@ -33,14 +33,12 @@ load helpers

     # Tagged import
     run_podman import -q $archive $fqin
-    iid="$output"
     run_podman run -t --rm $fqin cat /random.txt
     is "$output" "$random_content" "tagged import"
     run_podman rmi -f $fqin

     # Tagged import via stdin
-    run_podman import -q - $fqin < $archive
-    iid="$output"
+    run_podman import -q - $fqin < <(cat $archive)
     run_podman run -t --rm $fqin cat /random.txt
     is "$output" "$random_content" "tagged import via stdin"
     run_podman rmi -f $fqin
```
(I had forgotten one `<`).

With this fix, the test now fails in podman-without-your-patch:
```
podman run -t --rm <sha> cat /random.txt
   Error: executable file `cat` not found in $PATH: No such file or directory: OCI runtime attempted to invoke a command that was not found
```
